original:
The market is a 9, but the app is a 10

[Outpace Studios post — adapting format: meme pun ('she's a 10 but…') + product UI visual. 115 likes, 9.3K views. Number on screen matches caption.]

versions.label: A
versions.text: The AI space is a 9, but the interface is a 10.
versions.rationale: Direct format swap into Bricx's world. Works with any AI product UI screenshot. The meme reference lands even if you don't know the original.

versions.label: B
versions.text: The market's bearish. The design isn't.
versions.rationale: 6 words. Picks up the fear & greed context from the original — bearish sentiment vs premium-looking product. Contradiction compressed in two sentences (Rule 18). Pairs with a dark-mode UI visual.

versions.label: C
versions.rationale: 4 words. Startup context — early-stage companies are expected to look unpolished. Bricx breaks that assumption. No explanation needed. Let the product visual prove it (Rule 2). Sharpest version.
